title: Bitter Velvety Syrian Plum Tomato Stir-fry
description: A gluten-free Syrian plum tomato dish featuring baby plum tomatoes that are roasted for a velvety texture before stir-frying with aromatic spices.
instructions: Wash and slice baby plum tomatoes into quarters. Finely chop red onion, garlic, and mint leaves.
Combine sliced plum tomatoes with chopped onions, garlic, and mint in a large bowl. Drizzle olive oil over the mixture and season with salt, pepper, and chili flakes if using.
Toss all ingredients well to coat evenly with olive oil and seasoning.
Spread the tomato mixture on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Roast for about 15-20 minutes until tomatoes are tender but not falling apart.
While tomatoes are roasting, prepare the stir-fry by slicing red onion into thin rounds.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced onions and cook for about 3-4 minutes until softened.
Return roasted tomato mixture to pan with any residual juices from roasting. Stir-fry the tomatoes, onions, and mint together for another minute until heated through.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course accompaniment.
